Name: Barbell Bench Press
Description: Lie on a bench and press a barbell upward from chest level
Benefits: Builds chest mass and upper body strength

Name: Push-Up
Description: Lower your body to the floor and push back up with arms
Benefits: Strengthens chest shoulders and triceps no equipment needed

Name: Incline Dumbbell Press
Description: Press dumbbells upward on an inclined bench
Benefits: Targets upper chest and front deltoids

Name: Cable Fly
Description: Bring cable handles together in a hugging motion
Benefits: Isolates pectoral muscles with constant tension

Name: Dips
Description: Lower and raise body using parallel bars
Benefits: Develops lower chest and triceps

Name: Barbell Deadlift
Description: Lift a loaded barbell from the floor to hip level
Benefits: Builds full posterior chain strength and mass

Name: Pull-Up
Description: Hang from a bar and pull body up until chin clears
Benefits: Develops lat width and upper back strength

Name: Barbell Row
Description: Hinge forward and row barbell to lower chest
Benefits: Builds mid-back thickness and biceps

Name: Lat Pulldown
Description: Pull a cable bar down to upper chest while seated
Benefits: Great lat builder especially for beginners

Name: Seated Cable Row
Description: Pull cable handle toward abdomen while seated upright
Benefits: Builds mid-back and improves posture

Name: Overhead Press
Description: Press a barbell overhead from shoulder height
Benefits: Develops overall shoulder mass and strength

Name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
Description: Raise dumbbells out to the sides to shoulder height
Benefits: Isolates lateral deltoid for shoulder width

Name: Face Pull
Description: Pull rope attachment toward face with elbows high
Benefits: Strengthens rear delts and improves shoulder health

Name: Arnold Press
Description: Rotate dumbbells while pressing overhead
Benefits: Works all three heads of the deltoid

Name: Shrugs
Description: Lift shoulders straight up while holding weights
Benefits: Builds trapezius and upper back

Name: Barbell Bicep Curl
Description: Curl a barbell up toward shoulders
Benefits: Classic bicep mass builder

Name: Tricep Pushdown
Description: Push cable bar downward extending arms fully
Benefits: Isolates triceps for definition and size

Name: Hammer Curl
Description: Curl dumbbells with neutral wrist position
Benefits: Builds brachialis and overall arm thickness

Name: Skull Crusher
Description: Lower barbell to forehead from extended arms
Benefits: Excellent tricep mass builder

Name: Diamond Push-Up
Description: Push-up with hands close together in diamond shape
Benefits: Emphasizes triceps with no equipment needed

Name: Plank
Description: Hold a rigid straight-body position on forearms
Benefits: Builds core stability and endurance

Name: Crunch
Description: Curl upper body off floor toward knees
Benefits: Targets rectus abdominis directly

Name: Russian Twist
Description: Rotate torso side to side while seated with feet raised
Benefits: Works obliques and rotational core strength

Name: Hanging Leg Raise
Description: Hang from a bar and raise legs to 90 degrees
Benefits: Builds lower abs and hip flexors

Name: Cable Woodchop
Description: Pull cable diagonally across body in chopping motion
Benefits: Develops obliques and functional rotational power

Name: Barbell Squat
Description: Lower body until thighs are parallel to floor with bar on back
Benefits: King of leg exercises for mass and strength

Name: Romanian Deadlift
Description: Hinge at hips lowering bar along legs while keeping back flat
Benefits: Builds hamstrings and glutes

Name: Leg Press
Description: Push platform away with legs from seated position
Benefits: Safe quad and glute builder for beginners

Name: Lunges
Description: Step forward and lower back knee toward the floor
Benefits: Improves leg strength balance and coordination

Name: Calf Raise
Description: Rise onto toes while standing on edge of platform
Benefits: Isolates gastrocnemius and soleus

Name: Running
Description: Sustained jogging or running at moderate to high intensity
Benefits: Improves cardiovascular fitness burns calories and boosts mood

Name: Cycling
Description: Pedal a stationary or road bike at sustained effort
Benefits: Low-impact cardio excellent for leg endurance

Name: Rowing Machine
Description: Drive legs pull cable and lean back in rowing motion
Benefits: Full-body cardio that also builds back and core

Name: Jump Rope
Description: Continuously jump over a rotating rope
Benefits: Highly efficient cardio improves coordination and agility

Name: Burpee
Description: Combine squat jump push-up and jump in one movement
Benefits: High-intensity full-body conditioning exercise

Name: Mountain Climber
Description: Drive knees toward chest alternately in plank position
Benefits: Elevates heart rate while targeting core

Name: High Knees
Description: Run in place driving knees to hip height rapidly
Benefits: Burns calories and improves running mechanics

Name: Box Jump
Description: Jump from the floor landing softly on a raised box
Benefits: Develops explosive lower-body power

Name: Jumping Jacks
Description: Jump feet apart while raising arms overhead then return
Benefits: Simple effective warm-up and cardio exercise

Name: Battle Ropes
Description: Create waves in heavy ropes with alternating arm movements
Benefits: Intense conditioning builds upper body endurance

Name: Yoga Sun Salutation
Description: Flow through a sequence of linked yoga poses
Benefits: Improves flexibility balance and mind-body connection

Name: Foam Rolling IT Band
Description: Slowly roll outer thigh along a foam roller
Benefits: Releases tight IT band and reduces knee pain

Name: Hip Flexor Stretch
Description: Kneel and lunge forward to stretch front of hip
Benefits: Counteracts sitting and reduces lower back pain

Name: Child's Pose
Description: Kneel and reach arms forward while lowering chest to floor
Benefits: Gently stretches back hips and thighs

Name: Pigeon Pose
Description: Extend one leg behind while folding forward over bent leg
Benefits: Deep hip opener especially for piriformis

Name: Kettlebell Swing
Description: Hinge at hips and drive a kettlebell forward with hip extension
Benefits: Builds posterior chain power and cardiovascular conditioning

Name: TRX Row
Description: Pull body toward anchor point using suspension straps
Benefits: Scalable row that builds back and core stability

Name: Sled Push
Description: Drive a weighted sled forward with powerful leg drive
Benefits: Brutal conditioning that builds lower body and work capacity

Name: Wall Sit
Description: Hold squat position with back against a wall
Benefits: Builds quad endurance and mental toughness

Name: Farmer's Walk
Description: Carry heavy dumbbells by sides while walking
Benefits: Builds grip strength core stability and full body conditioning
